qwen2.5:7b

You're running an unsigned public beta of The Civic Desk. Windows SmartScreen may warn on install; that's expected. Report issues on GitHub.

Story Workbench

Modify drafted content, review guardrail warnings, and link source material when it helps the editor.

Back to Queue
Save Draft
Delete
Advisory warnings - these do not block publishing.
3 ISSUE(S)
Warns
- Review this guardrail issue

This reads like evergreen background or a source-intake note. Confirm what changed, why it matters now, and whether there is a real current story before publishing.

Technical details
Warns
- Review this guardrail issue

The linked lead is marked as watch. Confirm a current, specific, verified development before treating this as a reader-facing news story.

Technical details
Warns
- Consider adding a source link

This passage looks like a factual claim without a linked source. Add a link if one exists, rewrite the claim, or leave it for the editor to approve.

Technical details
Press-freedom / legal-risk advisor

Advisory only. Run this when you want risk notes, verification paths, and wording options. The publisher decides what to investigate, edit, hold, or publish.

Run Advisor
Distinguish verified facts, allegations, opinion, and unanswered questions.
Use extra care with private individuals, minors, medical details, addresses, and active legal matters.
Keep notes on source reliability and why publishing serves the community.
Story Title
Article Body (Markdown)
Plain Language Rewrite
Highlight text and click "Cite" in evidence pane to link.
Current Status: Sent back / needs work
Resume Editing
Mark Ready for Review
Hold
Cut Story
Approve for Static Publish
This draft was sent back for more work. Keep editing, add evidence, then mark it ready for review when it is ready for an editor again.
I have reviewed this story and take responsibility for publishing it. This reminder is for the editor; the app does not make the publication decision.
Social Media Promo Pack
Generate Posts

Generate optimized posts for Twitter/X, Facebook, and Reddit based on the current draft.

Welcome to the City of Longmont’s Public Information Department. Our mission is to ensure clear and timely communication between the city government and residents. We handle media relations, public announcements, and community outreach to keep you informed about municipal operations, initiatives, and events.

Stay updated with the latest information from the City of Longmont. Access press releases, city news, public records, and other essential resources to keep you informed about what’s happening in your community.
